name: White wine (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of White wine [Beverages] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: 19074160, 21371950, 3271892, Alicia Robles, Magdalena Fabjanowicz, Tomasz Chmiel, Justyna Płotka-Wasylka. Determination and identification of organic acids in wine samples. Problems and challenges. Trends in Analytical Chemistry 120 (2019) 115630. doi: 10.1016/j.trac.2019.115630, D. KOMES et al. Study of phenolic and volatile composition of white wine during fermentation. Vitis 46 (2), 77-84 (2007), Guo Cheng, Ye Liu, Tai-Xin Yue and Zhen-Wen Zhang. Comparison between aroma compounds in wines from four Vitis vinifera grape varieties grown in different shoot positions. Food Sci. Technol, Campinas, 35(2): 237-246, Apr.-Jun. 2015. http://dx.doi.org/10.1590/1678-457X.6438 , Leo P. Mccloskey. Gluconic Acid in California Wines. Am J Enol Vitic. January 1974 25: 198-201; published ahead of print January 01, 1974, Pablo Estévez,  M Luisa Gil and Elena Falqué. Effects of seven yeast strains on the volatile composition of Palomino wines. International Journal of Food Science & Technology, 2004, 39, Issue 1, p.61-69.  https://doi.org/10.1046/j.0950-5423.2003.00755.x.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
